content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

All three amendments identify real correctness defects. `fr-1-0b213bd9` and `fr-1-929f7a9f` overlap on the unconditional snapshot-based `plan_hash` fill that breaks idempotent replay after plan evolution, with `fr-1-929f7a9f` covering the broader service-layer root cause and start-path impact. `fr-1-b656ac14` independently identifies a real `started_at` matching gap in the runless fallback path.

## Votes

- `fr-1-0b213bd9`: Accept
  The defect is real. `record_bead_completion`/`update_task_run` replaces an omitted `plan_hash` with the current `snapshot.plan_hash` before lineage matching, so if an attempt was stored under `hash_v1` and `persist_plan` later advances the snapshot to `hash_v2`, replaying the same terminal completion with `plan_hash=None` reaches `src/adapters/fs.rs:3159-3161` as `Some(hash_v2)` and fails `option_conflicts(Some(hash_v1), Some(hash_v2))`. That breaks idempotent terminal replay for partial-write recovery. The fix should preserve detection of explicitly supplied conflicting hashes, but the reported bug itself is valid.
- `fr-1-b656ac14`: Accept
  Confirmed. In the `run_id` fallback branch at `src/adapters/fs.rs:2993-3023`, the adapter will select a sole open runless row without checking `started_at`. That is inconsistent with the no-`run_id` branch at `src/adapters/fs.rs:3077-3102`, the model helper in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:461-478`, and the service contract in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:466-469` that says `started_at` is forwarded to replay the correct runless attempt deterministically. As written, a named completion with the wrong timestamp can finalize the wrong legacy attempt. Requiring `entries[*index].started_at == started_at` and adding a regression test is the right change.
- `fr-1-929f7a9f`: Accept
  This is the broader root cause. Both `record_bead_start` and `update_task_run` unconditionally do `plan_hash.or_else(|| snapshot.plan_hash.clone())` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:431-433` and `566-568` before the lineage layer determines whether the call is creating a new row, backfilling a missing hash, or replaying an existing attempt. After `persist_plan` advances `snapshot.plan_hash`, replays against rows that already carry the older hash can then trip the conflict checks in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2711-2725` or `3159-3201`. Deferring the fallback until after matching, or applying it only when the matched/new row lacks `plan_hash`, is the correct direction, and replay coverage across a later plan update is justified.
